productOverview.oneLiner: Solvyn is a luxury mineral sunscreen brand delivering FDA-approved, single-use SPF 50 protection for hotels, resorts, events, and everyday sun exposure.
productOverview.whatItDoes: Shield by Solvyn is a 100% mineral, broad-spectrum SPF formulated with non-nano zinc oxide and packaged in a sealed, single-use pouch. Designed for high-exposure hospitality environments — not bathroom shelves.
productOverview.productType: Physical product (consumer packaged goods) — B2B distribution + D2C waitlist

targetAudience.targetCompanies: Hotels, resorts, spas, wellness retreats, event venues, stadiums
targetAudience.decisionMakers: Hospitality operations managers, guest experience directors, spa managers, event coordinators, procurement teams
targetAudience.primaryUseCase: Providing guests with convenient, hygienic, premium sunscreen as a venue amenity
jobsToBeDone: Elevate guest experience with a memorable "save the day" amenity
Eliminate hygiene concerns of shared sunscreen bottles
Reduce operational mess (no dripping pumps, less linen staining)
Meet sustainability/ESG goals with reef-safe, compliant products
targetAudience.secondaryAudience: Individual consumers who want premium mineral sunscreen for travel, outdoor activities, everyday use — currently a waitlist

personas.challenge: Sunscreen is overlooked as an amenity; shared bottles are unhygienic
personas.valuePromise: Premium guest-facing amenity that elevates experience

personas.caresAbout: Product quality, clean ingredients, guest safety
personas.challenge: Finding products that align with wellness brand standards
Outdoor events lack sun protection; bulky bottles are impractical
personas.valuePromise: Single-use pouches = zero mess, easy distribution

personas.challenge: Chemical sunscreens stain linens; bulky packaging creates waste
personas.valuePromise: Reduces replacement costs, minimal waste, reef-safe compliance

painPoints.coreProblem: Sun exposure happens everywhere — but sunscreen isn't always available, trusted, or convenient. Venues lack a premium, hygienic sunscreen solution for guests.
whyAlternativesFallShort: Bulk sunscreens are messy (dripping, spilling, staining linens)
Chemical sunscreens damage reefs and aren't regulation-compliant
Generic drugstore brands don't match luxury venue branding
painPoints.whatItCosts: Linen replacement from sunscreen stains, poor guest reviews, housekeeping labor, missed guest satisfaction opportunities
painPoints.emotionalTension: Guests feel unprotected and frustrated; venues feel they're offering a subpar experience

secondary: Bulk sunscreen dispensers/pump stations at pools
Generic drugstore sunscreen brands offered at gift shops
indirect: Venues choosing not to offer sunscreen at all
competitiveLandscape.howEachFallsShort: Bulk = unhygienic, messy, wasteful. Gift shop brands = not premium, chemical-based, not branded to venue. No sunscreen = missed opportunity, liability risk.

keyDifferentiators: Single-use sealed pouch format (hygienic, portable, no mess)
FDA broad-spectrum SPF, dermatologist formulated & approved
differentiation.howDifferently: Built from the ground up for venues, not repurposed from retail
differentiation.whyBetter: Hygiene, convenience, brand elevation, compliance, and cost savings in one product
differentiation.whyChooseUs: The only sunscreen that feels like a luxury amenity, not an afterthought

items.response: Is it sealed, single-use, mineral, and reef-safe? Does it elevate your guest experience or just check a box?
Single-use pouches actually reduce waste vs. half-used bottles left poolside. Plus our formula is reef-safe and packaging is eco-conscious.

items.objection: It's too expensive for an amenity
items.response: Shield reduces linen replacement costs from chemical sunscreen stains and becomes a guest-satisfaction differentiator that drives reviews and loyalty.

objections.antiPersona: Budget motels/hostels looking for cheapest option; brands that don't care about ingredient quality; venues with no outdoor/sun exposure

switchingDynamics.push: Shared bottles feel unhygienic post-COVID; chemical sunscreens staining expensive linens; guest complaints about lack of sun protection
switchingDynamics.pull: Premium look/feel that matches venue branding; sealed pouches = zero contamination; reef-safe compliance for coastal properties
switchingDynamics.habit: "We've always just put out a pump bottle" — inertia of existing approach
switchingDynamics.anxiety: "Will guests actually use a pouch format?" "Is it worth the per-unit cost?"

problemDescriptions: Guests are always asking for sunscreen
The pump bottles by the pool are gross
We need something that feels premium, not drugstore
customerLanguage.wordsToUse: Protection, mineral, clean, dermatologist-trusted, reef-safe, premium, elevated, effortless, convenient, sealed, hygienic
customerLanguage.wordsToAvoid: Cheap, sunblock, lotion, basic, product (say "our sunscreen" or "Shield by Solvyn"), chemical

glossary.meaning: Solvyn's hero product — mineral SPF 50 sunscreen
Mineral UV filter that doesn't penetrate skin
Protects against both UVA and UVB rays

brandVoice.tone: Calm luxury — confident, warm, premium without being pretentious
brandVoice.style: Direct but elegant. Editorial feel. Let the words breathe. No urgency, no hard sell.

proofPoints.customers: Amrit Resort (Founding Partner), Hollywood Dermatology and Cosmetic Surgery Specialists, American Society for Mohs Surgery
proofPoints.medicalCredibility: Dr. Eduardo Weiss, MD — Board-Certified Dermatologist, Fellow FAAD, 30+ years clinical experience, professor at FIU and University of Miami Miller School of Medicine

valueThemes.proof: Single-use pouch designed for venues, not retail shelves
